questions.question: Explain the working principle of Token Passing protocol with a neat diagram.
keyPoints: Token is a special frame that circulates among all nodes
Only the node possessing the token can transmit
After transmission, node releases the token to the next node
Ensures collision-free operation

questions.question: What are the advantages of controlled access protocols over random access protocols?
keyPoints: No collisions occur
Predictable and guaranteed bandwidth
Suitable for real-time applications
Fair access to all nodes

questions.question: Describe the three states of a node in Token Ring network operation.
keyPoints: Transmit state: When node holds the token and can transmit data
Receive state: When node receives frames addressed to it
Pass state: When node simply forwards token/frames to next node

questions.question: Explain the polling mechanism in controlled access protocols.
keyPoints: Central controller (primary) controls all communications
Primary sends poll frames to each secondary node sequentially
Only polled node can transmit data
Response can be positive (data) or negative (no data)

questions.question: What is token holding time (THT) and why is it important?
keyPoints: THT is maximum time a node can hold the token
Prevents any single node from monopolizing channel
Ensures fair access to all nodes
Typically set to 10ms in Token Ring

questions.question: Explain the difference between Token Ring and Token Bus protocols.
keyPoints: Token Ring uses physical ring topology; Token Bus uses physical bus topology
Both implement logical ring for token passing
Token Bus uses MAC addresses to maintain logical ring order
IEEE 802.5 defines Token Ring; IEEE 802.4 defines Token Bus

questions.question: How is token loss handled in Token Ring networks?
keyPoints: Active Monitor detects token loss by timeout
Claim token process is initiated by stations
Stations compete to become new Active Monitor
New token is generated after election

questions.question: With the help of a block diagram, explain the Token Ring operation including frame formats, token handling, and recovery mechanisms.
keyPoints: Block diagram showing ring topology with monitor
Token and frame formats (3-byte token, variable data frames)
Four states of ring operation: transmit, receive, bypass, claim
Token loss detection by TRT timeout
Active Monitor functions and token regeneration
Beaconing for fault isolation
Priority reservation mechanism

questions.question: Compare Token Passing, Polling, and Reservation-based controlled access protocols. Discuss their advantages, disadvantages, and suitable applications.
keyPoints: Token Passing: distributed, no central failure, collision-free, suitable for LANs
Polling: centralized, simple, single point of failure, suitable for SCADA/industrial
Reservation: flexible, good for satellite/wireless, overhead for reservation
Comparison table of performance metrics
Applications: Token Ring/Bus for LAN, Polling for industrial control, Reservation for satellite/mobile

questions.question: Explain the working of Token Bus (IEEE 802.4) including logical ring maintenance, token handling, and frame transmission.
keyPoints: Physical bus/tree topology with logical ring
MAC addresses used to maintain logical ring order (descending)
Successor and predecessor addresses stored in each node
Token contains logical link addresses for routing
Three types: broadband, carrierband, and fiber optic
Token recovery procedures similar to Token Ring
Advantages over Token Ring: bus topology, better reliability

questions.question: Describe the various frame types in Token Ring and explain the priority and reservation mechanism.
keyPoints: Token frame (3 bytes)
Data/Command frame structure (starting delimiter, access control, frame control, data, FCS, ending delimiter)
Frame status field with A and C bits
Priority system: 3-bit priority field in token
Reservation: nodes can request priority for next token
Priority escalation for high-priority traffic
Example scenarios showing priority handling
